data.text: The review model already supports structured per-AC evidence in `ReviewResult.PerAC`, so the new gate can be keyed off that instead of inventing a new schema. I’m now checking how that field is produced and whether the execute loop already has the right data flow to require two evidence-backed APPROVE slots before calling `CloseWithEvidence`.

I’ve narrowed the behavior change: the loop should not close first and then react to review verdicts. Instead, it needs a gate that requires a unanimous, evidence-backed APPROVE decision before any close call, while REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK only emit review records and leave the bead open. I’m checking the existing `PerAC` semantics now so the gate matches the established review schema rather than inventing a new notion of “evidence-backed.”
